words.def: an official way of showing who you are, for example a document with your name, date of birth and often a photograph on it (the abbreviation for ‘identity’ or ‘identification’)
words.example: You must carry ID at all times.

words.def: perfect; most suitable
words.example: an ideal location/place

words.def: similar in every detail
words.example: a row of identical houses

words.def: to recognize somebody/something and be able to say who or what they are
words.example: identify somebody/something, She was able to identify her attacker.

words.def: a false idea or belief, especially about somebody or about a situation
words.example: under the illusion that…, She's under the illusion that (= believes wrongly that) she'll get the job.

words.def: to use pictures, photographs, diagrams, etc. in a book, etc.
words.example: illustrate something, She illustrated her own books.

words.def: a drawing or picture in a book, magazine, for decoration or to explain something
words.example: 50 full-colour illustrations

words.def: the ability to create pictures in your mind; the part of your mind that does this
words.example: a vivid/fertile imagination

words.def: a person who has come to live permanently in a different country from the one they were born in
words.example: immigrant from…, immigrants from other European countries

words.def: the process of coming to live permanently in a different country from the one you were born in; the number of people who do this
words.example: legal/illegal immigration

words.def: that cannot catch or be affected by a particular disease or illness
words.example: Adults are often immune to German measles.

words.def: annoyed by somebody/something, especially because you have to wait for a long time
words.example: I'd been waiting for twenty minutes and I was getting impatient.

words.def: to make something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
words.example: to implement changes/decisions/policies/reforms

words.def: a possible effect or result of an action or a decision
words.example: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions.

words.def: to suggest that something is true or that you feel or think something, without saying so directly
words.example: imply (that)…, Are you implying (that) I am wrong?

words.def: a product or service that is brought into one country from another
words.example: import from…, food imports from abroad

words.def: to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to order that a rule, punishment, etc. be used
words.example: impose something, The UN Security Council imposed sanctions in 1992.

words.def: if a person or thing impresses you, you admire them or it
words.example: impress (somebody), We interviewed a number of candidates but none of them impressed us.

words.def: admiring somebody/something because you think they are particularly good, interesting, etc.
words.example: I must admit I am impressed.

words.def: something that encourages you to do something
words.example: There is no incentive for people to save fuel.

words.def: a unit for measuring length or height, equal to 2.54 centimetres. There are 12 inches in a foot.
words.example: She's a few inches taller than me.

words.def: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant
words.example: His bad behaviour was just an isolated incident.

words.def: the money that a person, a region, a country, etc. earns from work, from investing money, from business, etc.
words.example: Average household income fell slightly.

words.def: to include something so that it forms a part of something
words.example: incorporate something, The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.

words.def: not accurate or true
words.example: incorrect information/spelling

words.def: more and more all the time
words.example: Tourism is playing an increasingly important role in the region's economy

words.def: freedom from political control by other countries
words.example: Cuba gained independence from Spain in 1898.

words.def: a list of names or topics that are referred to in a book, etc., usually arranged at the end of a book in alphabetical order or listed in a separate file or book
words.example: Look it up in the index.

words.def: a remark or sign that shows that something is happening or what somebody is thinking or feeling
words.example: indication of something, They gave no indication of how the work should be done.

words.def: connected with industry
words.example: an industrial dispute

words.def: that you cannot avoid or prevent
words.example: It was an inevitable consequence of the decision.

words.def: as is certain to happen
words.example: Inevitably, the press exaggerated the story.

words.def: the act or process of causing or getting a disease
words.example: to cause/prevent infection

words.def: to reach an opinion or decide that something is true on the basis of information that is available
words.example: infer something (from something), Much of the meaning must be inferred from the context.

words.def: a general rise in the prices of services and goods in a particular country, resulting in a fall in the value of money; the rate at which this happens
words.example: the fight against rising inflation

words.def: information
words.example: For more info, click here.

words.def: to tell somebody about something, especially in an official way
words.example: inform somebody, The government took this decision without consulting Parliament or informing the public.

words.def: relaxed and friendly; not following strict rules of how to behave or do something
words.example: an informal atmosphere

words.def: a person or an animal that lives in a particular place
words.example: the oldest inhabitant of the village

words.def: to receive money, property, etc. from somebody when they die
words.example: inherit something from somebody, She inherited a fortune from her father.

words.def: happening at the beginning; first
words.example: There is an initial payment of £60 followed by ten instalments of £25.

words.def: at the beginning
words.example: Initially, the system worked well.

words.def: a new plan for dealing with a particular problem or for achieving a particular purpose
words.example: a United Nations peace initiative

words.def: physically hurt; having an injury
words.example: His injured leg prevented him from walking.

words.def: coloured liquid for writing, drawing and printing
words.example: in ink, written in ink

words.def: inside; towards or close to the centre of a place
words.example: an inner courtyard

words.def: the introduction of new things, ideas or ways of doing something
words.example: an age of technological innovation

words.def: introducing or using new ideas, ways of doing something, etc.
words.example: There will be a prize for the most innovative design.

words.def: time, knowledge, ideas, etc. that you put into work, a project, etc. in order to make it succeed; the act of putting something in
words.example: I'd appreciate your input on this.

words.def: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something
words.example: a murder inquiry

words.def: to put something into something else or between two things
words.example: insert something (in/into something), Insert coins into the slot and press for a ticket.

words.def: forming the inner part of something; not on the outside
words.example: the inside pages of a newspaper

words.def: an understanding of what something is like
words.example: There are many valuable insights in her book.

words.def: to demand that something happen or that somebody agree to do something
words.example: I didn't really want to go but he insisted.

words.def: an officer of middle rank in the police force
words.example: Inspector Maggie Forbes

words.def: to give somebody the desire, confidence or enthusiasm to do something well
words.example: inspire somebody, The actors' enthusiasm inspired the kids.

words.def: to fix equipment or furniture into position so that it can be used
words.example: They're planning to install a new drainage system.

words.def: the act of fixing equipment or furniture in position so that it can be used
words.example: installation costs

words.def: a particular example or case of something
words.example: instance of somebody/something, The report highlights a number of instances of injustice.

words.def: happening immediately
words.example: This account gives you instant access to your money.
